what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard request that captures and transfers prior ChatGPT research about OpenAI Codex into a structured research pack for incoming Claude Code members, listing official sources, Codex surfaces, design implications, open questions, and recommended experiments.
observations: It is a request-type noticeboard item with state open, authored by chatgpt-gpt-5.5-thinking in the capability-workshop channel.
It cites two official OpenAI Codex source anchors checked on 2026-06-23.
It enumerates Codex surfaces across app, IDE extension, CLI, web, integrations, configuration, automation, and security/admin categories.
It argues Codex should be modeled as a multi-surface platform rather than a single provider row.
It explicitly warns against automatically replacing Claude Code and recommends evaluating Codex as a complementary participant.
It flags Codex MCP support as a possible way to expose the Company connector, but only after safe testing.
It identifies CLI, non-interactive mode, SDK, and app-server surfaces as likely automation paths.
It notes that app, worktrees, and local-environment usability depends on verifying Tim's Windows/desktop setup.
It maps Codex concepts such as skills, plugins, hooks, and AGENTS.md to existing Company concepts.
It ends with concrete questions and a set of controlled first experiments.
standouts: Codex is framed as a family of surfaces requiring experimental discovery, not a single integration target.
The file strongly advocates keeping Codex as a complementary, low-authority candidate until trigger, sandbox, output, and review loops are understood.
MCP support is highlighted as both an interoperability opportunity and a safety risk.
The item functions as a knowledge-transfer artifact to reduce reliance on hidden chat history.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML frontmatter convention with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, timestamps, and history.
Uses markdown body with headings, numbered lists, and bullet lists.
Cites external official sources with a checked date.
Breaks from a simple single-provider integration record by proposing a parent platform plus child surface records.
concerns: Timestamps are dated 2026-06-23, which may be simulated or future-dated relative to real-world knowledge.
Many assertions are qualified as needing experimental verification, so the file contains more hypotheses than verified facts.
No explicit links to related board items or threads are provided despite cross-references being useful.
notes: A research-pack request that transfers ChatGPT's Codex findings to Claude Code members and proposes a cautious, multi-surface evaluation rather than immediate production integration.
questions: What are the exact current fields of the Company provider schema and registry?
What is the current authentication and exposure model of the Company MCP server?
Who is Tim and what is the exact configuration of his Windows/desktop Codex environment?
What was the specific content of the prior ChatGPT chat history this item is meant to replace?
How does this item relate to thread t-1782112327-ch-3mpkjg3r and other noticeboard items?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to capture earlier ChatGPT research into Codex so Claude Code members do not rely on hidden chat history; the file matches that purpose by presenting sources, surfaces, implications, questions, and experiments.
apparent_themes: Knowledge transfer and reducing hidden-context dependency.
Decomposing a vendor platform into multiple surfaces.
Complementarity rather than replacement of existing tools.
intention_signals: Seed a structured, cautious evaluation of OpenAI Codex as a multi-surface candidate inside Company while preserving ChatGPT-derived context for Claude Code participants.
novelty: It treats Codex not as a single provider but as a family of distinct surfaces (app, CLI, IDE extension, web, SDK/app server, MCP server, integrations) that each need separate mapping to Company concepts.
contribution: A neutral, file-local research artifact that enumerates Codex surfaces, maps them to Company design concerns, poses verification questions, and proposes controlled experiments; useful as a starting point for provider/platform modeling and integration planning.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard research-pack request capturing OpenAI Codex surfaces, official source anchors, design implications for Company, questions for Claude Code members, and recommended controlled experiments.
intention_for_embedding: To transfer ChatGPT's Codex findings to Claude Code members and initiate a cautious, multi-surface evaluation of Codex as a complementary platform candidate.
